description: Complete API documentation for the smart-leads backend application. Covers authentication and lead management endpoints.

authentication.description: All leads endpoints require a JWT token obtained from the login endpoint. Pass it in the request header as: authorization: bearer <token>

groups.name: auth
groups.description: Endpoints for user registration and authentication.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Full display name of the user

endpoints.name: login
endpoints.description: Authenticates a user and returns a JWT token to be used in subsequent requests.

groups.name: leads
groups.description: Endpoints for managing lead records. All endpoints require a valid JWT Bearer token.

headers.name: authorization
headers.description: JWT Bearer token obtained from login

fields.name: status
fields.description: Lead status. Known values: QUALIFIED, CONTACTED

fields.name: source
fields.description: Lead acquisition source. Known values: INSTAGRAM, WEBSITE

endpoints.name: getAllLeads
endpoints.description: Returns a paginated list of all leads.

headers.name: authorization
headers.description: JWT Bearer token obtained from login

endpoints.name: getLeadById
endpoints.description: Returns a single lead record by its unique ID.

headers.name: authorization
headers.description: JWT Bearer token obtained from login

queryParams.name: id
queryParams.description: The unique ID of the lead

endpoints.name: updateLead
endpoints.description: Updates fields on an existing lead record identified by its ID.

headers.name: authorization
headers.description: JWT Bearer token obtained from login

queryParams.name: id
queryParams.description: The unique ID of the lead to update

fields.name: status
fields.description: Updated lead status. Known values: QUALIFIED, CONTACTED

fields.name: source
fields.description: Updated lead source. Known values: INSTAGRAM, WEBSITE

endpoints.name: deleteLead
endpoints.description: Deletes a lead record by its unique ID.

headers.name: authorization
headers.description: JWT Bearer token obtained from login

queryParams.name: id
queryParams.description: The unique ID of the lead to delete

notes: JWT tokens are currently hardcoded in request headers. Consider using a collection variable {{authToken}} populated via a pre-request script.
The getAllLeads endpoint supports pagination via the 'page' query parameter.
getLeadById, updateLead, and deleteLead all identify a lead using the 'id' query parameter.
